aspirin.description: Concurrent use significantly increases the risk of major bleeding events including gastrointestinal hemorrhage and intracranial bleeding.

ibuprofen.description: NSAIDs reduce warfarin metabolism and increase anticoagulant effect, leading to high bleeding risk.

naproxen.description: Naproxen combined with warfarin greatly increases risk of GI bleeding.

acetaminophen.description: High doses of acetaminophen (>2g/day) may enhance anticoagulant effect of warfarin.

ciprofloxacin.description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, increasing warfarin levels and bleeding risk.

fluconazole.description: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, dramatically increasing warfarin levels.

amoxicillin.description: Some antibiotics may alter gut flora affecting vitamin K production and warfarin efficacy.

ibuprofen.description: NSAIDs may reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors and increase renal risk.

potassium.description: ACE inhibitors increase potassium retention; supplements may cause dangerous hyperkalemia.

spironolactone.description: Both increase potassium levels, risking life-threatening hyperkalemia.

naproxen.description: NSAIDs reduce effectiveness of ACE inhibitors and may worsen kidney function.

alcohol.description: Alcohol potentiates metformin's effect on lactate metabolism, increasing lactic acidosis risk.

contrast_dye.description: Iodinated contrast with metformin increases lactic acidosis risk. Stop metformin 48h before.

ciprofloxacin.description: Ciprofloxacin may alter blood glucose levels when combined with metformin.

clarithromycin.description: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, dramatically increasing statin levels and rhabdomyolysis risk.

grapefruit.description: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4 and may increase statin blood levels.

gemfibrozil.description: Fibrates combined with statins significantly increase myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk.

verapamil.description: Both drugs slow heart rate and AV conduction; combination may cause severe bradycardia or heart block.

clonidine.description: Abrupt discontinuation of either drug can cause rebound hypertension crisis.

fluoxetine.description: Fluoxetine inhibits CYP2D6, increasing metoprolol levels and risk of bradycardia.

tramadol.description: Both increase serotonin; combination risks serotonin syndrome (hyperthermia, agitation, tremor).

sumatriptan.description: Triptans with SSRIs increase serotonin syndrome risk.

warfarin.description: SSRIs may impair platelet aggregation and increase bleeding risk with anticoagulants.

clopidogrel.description: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, blocking clopidogrel activation and reducing antiplatelet effect.

methotrexate.description: PPIs may delay methotrexate elimination, increasing toxicity risk.

antacids.description: Aluminum/magnesium antacids chelate ciprofloxacin, reducing absorption by up to 90%.

theophylline.description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits theophylline metabolism, risking seizures and cardiac arrhythmias.

tizanidine.description: Ciprofloxacin increases tizanidine levels 10-fold, causing severe hypotension and sedation.

warfarin.description: High doses of paracetamol (>2g/day) may enhance the anticoagulant effect of warfarin, increasing INR and bleeding risk.

alcohol.description: Paracetamol with alcohol significantly increases the risk of severe hepatotoxicity and liver failure.

isoniazid.description: Isoniazid may increase paracetamol hepatotoxicity by inducing CYP2E1 enzyme.

warfarin.description: Mefenamic acid increases anticoagulant effect and risk of gastrointestinal bleeding with warfarin.

lithium.description: NSAIDs including mefenamic acid may increase lithium plasma levels, risking toxicity.

methotrexate.description: Mefenamic acid reduces renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity risk.

aspirin.description: Concurrent use of two NSAIDs increases risk of gastrointestinal ulceration and bleeding.

warfarin.description: Aceclofenac increases bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants.

lisinopril.description: NSAIDs may reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors.

warfarin.description: Nimesulide may enhance the anticoagulant effect of warfarin, increasing bleeding risk.

metformin.description: Concurrent use may affect blood glucose control.
